================================================================================ UPGCS CHATBOT KNOWLEDGE BASE University Post Graduate College, Secunderabad (Osmania University) ================================================================================ Version: 2024 | Source: www.upgcs.in | Format: Chatbot-Optimized Plain Text ================================================================================ ================================================================================ SECTION 1: COLLEGE IDENTITY ================================================================================ COLLEGE_NAME: University Post Graduate College, Secunderabad SHORT_NAME: UPGCS ALSO_KNOWN_AS: University PG College, OU Secunderabad AFFILIATED_TO: Osmania University, Hyderabad TYPE: Co-educational Constituent College ESTABLISHED: 1947 CITY: Secunderabad STATE: Telangana COUNTRY: India WEBSITE: www.upgcs.in HISTORY: - 1947: Founded as "Arts & Science College, Secunderabad" — UG courses only - 1989: Upgraded and renamed "Post-Graduate College, Secunderabad" — PG courses added - 2017: Renamed to "University Post Graduate College, OU Secunderabad" (UPGCS) ABOUT_COLLEGE:

renamed "Post-Graduate College, Secunderabad" — PG courses added - 2017: Renamed to "University Post Graduate College, OU Secunderabad" (UPGCS) ABOUT_COLLEGE: UPGCS is one of the best co-educational institutions of higher learning in the twin cities of Hyderabad and Secunderabad. It is a constituent college of Osmania University. The college offers both Under Graduate (UG) and Post Graduate (PG) courses. Most faculty hold PhD degrees. The college is a preferred destination for foreign students from Sudan, Yemen, Tanzania, Nigeria, Djibouti, Ethiopia and other African countries. The foremost objective is to nurture young minds, stimulate learning and mould their personalities. ABOUT_UNIVERSITY: Osmania

and other African countries. The foremost objective is to nurture young minds, stimulate learning and mould their personalities. ABOUT_UNIVERSITY: Osmania University (OU) was established in 1918 in Hyderabad, Telangana. It is one of the oldest and most prestigious universities in India — the 7th oldest in the country and 3rd oldest in South India. It is UGC-recognised and NAAC-accredited. It governs all academic programmes, examinations, and degree awards for UPGCS. ================================================================================ SECTION 2: VISION AND MISSION ================================================================================ VISION: We aim to strive and uphold the values of humanity, nurturing through education the next generation of nation builders. MISSION: 1. To

to strive and uphold the values of humanity, nurturing through education the next generation of nation builders. MISSION: 1. To impart quality education and create a healthy learning environment 2. To promote high standards of teaching and research, ethics, integrity and human values 3. To motivate students to acquire creative and critical skills 4. To inculcate the values and virtues of citizenship 5. To prepare students to meet the demands of new realms of global knowledge 6. Both boys and girls are eligible. - For B.Com (Computers) and B.Sc (MPC): Must have passed Intermediate (TS Board or equivalent). -

and girls are eligible. - For B.Com (Computers) and B.Sc (MPC): Must have passed Intermediate (TS Board or equivalent). - All Intermediate-passed students can apply for B.Com (Computers) regardless of subjects studied. - For B.Sc (MPC): Minimum 40% in optional subjects required. SC/ST candidates need only pass marks. - Compartmental/Instant exam candidates eligible if seats are available and documents submitted on time. PG_ELIGIBILITY: - Must appear for OUCET (Osmania University Common Entrance Test) / CPGET. - Admissions through centralised counselling based on OUCET merit. --- RESERVATIONS --- LOCAL_SEATS: 85% reserved for local candidates (Osmania University local area) NON_LOCAL_SEATS: 15% unreserved

based on OUCET merit. --- RESERVATIONS --- LOCAL_SEATS: 85% reserved for local candidates (Osmania University local area) NON_LOCAL_SEATS: 15% unreserved — open to both local and non-local candidates CATEGORY_RESERVATIONS: Backward Class (Total) — 25% BC Group-A — 7% BC Group-B — 10% BC Group-C — 1% BC Group-D — 7% Scheduled Caste (SC) — 15% Scheduled Tribe (ST) — 6% SPORTS_NCC_NSS_QUOTA: 1 seat per block of 30 seats (minimum 1 seat) per course — for Sports, NCC, NSS combined PHYSICALLY_HANDICAPPED_QUOTA: 1 seat per block of 30 seats (minimum 1 seat) per course WOMEN: Statutory reservation rules followed --- SCHOLARSHIPS ---

A: University Post Graduate College, Secunderabad (also called University PG College, OU Secunderabad). Q: When was UPGCS established? A: In 1947, the same year India gained independence. Q: Is UPGCS a government college? A: Yes. It

UPGCS established? A: In 1947, the same year India gained independence. Q: Is UPGCS a government college? A: Yes. It is a constituent college of Osmania University, a government university in Hyderabad, Telangana. Q: Is UPGCS co-educational? A: Yes. UPGCS is a co-educational institution open to both boys and girls. Q: What UG courses are offered? A: BBA, B.Com (Regular), B.Com (Computers), B.Sc (MPC), B.Sc (MPCs), BCA, B.A (HPP), B.A (EPP), B.Sc (Data Science), and B.Sc Honours (Computer Science). Q: What PG courses are offered? A: MA English, MA Hindi, MA Economics, MA Public Administration, MA History, MA Political Science,

What PG courses are offered? A: MA English, MA Hindi, MA Economics, MA Public Administration, MA History, MA Political Science, M.Com, MSW, MCA, and M.Sc Data Science. Q: How do I apply for UG admission? A: Through DOST — Degree Online Services, Telangana at https://dost.cgg.gov.in/ Q: How do I apply for PG admission? A: Through CPGET (Common Post-Graduate Entrance Test) at https://cpget.tsche.ac.in/ Q: How do I apply for MCA? A: Through ICET at https://icet.tsche.ac.in/ Q: What is the eligibility for B.Sc (MPC)? A: Must have passed Intermediate (TS Board or equivalent) with at least 40% in optional subjects. SC/ST need

Closed on Sundays and public holidays. Q: How many books are in the library? A: Over 79,000 books covering all major subjects. Q: What is IQAC? Yes. UPGCS offers NCC Air Wing. NCC Officer is Dr. Y. Mallaiah. Q: Does UPGCS have NSS? A: Yes. UPGCS has 2 NSS units. Unit 1 (Boys): Dr. Rajender Kandukuri; Unit 2 (Girls): Ms. A. Sandhya. Q: What sports facilities are available? A: Outdoor: Volleyball, Basketball, Badminton, Tennicoit. Indoor: Chess, Carrom. Sprawling grounds with floodlights and a large sports complex. firms, and government services (TSPSC, Police Board, Gurukula Board). Q: What scholarships are available? reservation for ST students? A: 6% of total seats. Q: What is the reservation for Backward Classes (BC)? A: 25% in total (BC-A: 7%, BC-B: 10%, BC-C: 1%, BC-D: 7%). Q: What second languages are available for UG students? A: Telugu, Hindi, and Sanskrit. Q: Does UPGCS accept international students? A: Yes. The college is a preferred destination for students from Sudan, Yemen, Tanzania, Nigeria, Djibouti, Ethiopia and other African countries. Q: What is the vision of UPGCS? strive and uphold the values of humanity, nurturing through education the next generation of nation builders. Q: What is the MSW programme? A: Master of Social Work — a 2-year PG programme.
